fullText: ai is overwhelming and this course is our humble effort to make it a little less intimidating and maybe have a little fun while we're at it My name is Pumshad Manhatt and I'll be your instructor for this course We start from the absolute basics of AI and work our way up to understanding what AI agents are Along the way we'll dissect OpenClaw a popular open-source AI agent that has gained a lot of interest recently to see exactly what made it tick But this isn't just another theory only course You'll get hands-on access to our lab environments where you practice building agents yourself stress-free without worrying about unexpected charges We provide everything you need from API keys to sandbox cloud environments so you can focus purely on learning Throughout this course you will build four AI agents I call them Zippy Savvy Meshi and Cody Pardon the choice of names I thought they'd go well with the with the vibe You'll see Zippy's journey from being a solo generic agent to becoming an orchestrator that manages a multi- aent setup of Savvy a research agent Meshi a memory agent and Kodi a coding agent Here's how the course is structured We start with fundamentals the building blocks of AI You'll learn what Chad GBP actually is how large language models work what tokens and tokenization mean how temperature and sampling shape responses and how to write effective prompts From there we move into architecture How AI systems are assembled from pods You'll learn what tools are and how to integrate them Building AI workflows and understand the key distinction that most people miss What separates a workflow from an agent Then comes implementation where you build real agents You'll learn what an agent actually is how agent memory works planning and reasoning strategies multi- aent systems and error handling patterns You'll also explore coding agents and the design patterns that Anthropic and Google use in production We close with the open claw case study where we dissect a real open source AI agent You'll walk through open clause architecture and tools its agent loop memory system testing strategies monitoring and security what's everyone's concern regarding OpenClaus security challenges is it real what caused it etc By the end you'll know how to build test and deploy effective AI agents from scratch
